vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding the current state of the biome. P1 (String of Nickels) is not visible as a living specimen. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct healthy plants. P3 (Pothos) is present but appears to be a singular, struggling cutting or remnant.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: White rabbit anchor remains, but plant material is minimal.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty/Soil only. No signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Empty/Soil only. No signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: Contains white rabbit anchor; minimal plant matter present.
plant_audit.P4_Shared_Black_Pot: Empty/Soil only. No signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, appears damp/saturated. No fungal blooms detected, but surface texture is uniform and devoid of active growth.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of a metallic spoon (T-2 to Current) and a white cylindrical object (Current) indicates human intervention/clutter.

temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Introduction of a metallic spoon in the yellow pot area.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Lighting shift; visibility reduced, but no new growth observed.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Introduction of a white cylindrical container/object near the yellow pot.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline or dormancy. The lack of visible foliage across all pots suggests that the plants are either dead, buried, or have been removed. The environment is currently being used for storage of non-botanical items (spoon, cylinder).
anomalies: White cylindrical object in P1/P2 area.
Absence of all registered plant life except for the P3 anchor.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection has suffered a catastrophic loss of biomass. Over the 5-day observation period, the pots have transitioned from potential plant containers to empty soil vessels. The presence of a spoon and a cylindrical object suggests the desk is no longer being prioritized for plant health, but rather as a utility surface. The P3 Pothos anchor remains, but the plant itself is not thriving.
